D1 OpticsL3 ApplyReference

Abbe Resolution Limit — d = λ / (2 NA)

Smallest resolvable period for a microscope: d = λ/(2·n·sin α) where NA = n sin α is numerical aperture; immersion oil (n ≈ 1.5) shrinks d by ~33%

Ernst Abbe (1873) showed: a microscope objective must collect at least the 0th + ±1st diffraction orders to reconstruct a periodic structure. At 550 nm with NA 1.4 (oil immersion), d ≈ 196 nm. STED, PALM, STORM "super-resolution" methods circumvent this — Nobel 2014.

Slide NA to see resolution limit; immersion required above NA 1.0.
